findings.problem: Narrative process leakage breaks diegesis: the line references prior events as "chapter-seven trust and chapter-nine panic," which reads like drafting metadata instead of in-world narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:201, replace chapter-number meta labeling with in-world recall anchored to concrete events (e.g., the specific trust exchange and Jamsil panic context) while preserving the emotional logic of why she uses that burner.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if line 201 no longer contains chapter-number/meta language and the burner reference is understandable entirely as in-world memory; FAIL if any chapter/cycle labeling remains in narration.

findings.problem: At the peak emotional/intimate decision point, prose retreats into euphemistic abstraction ("simple body answer", "used each other that way") instead of embodied specificity. This is sanitization drift that blunts stakes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:314-327 so Ina's temptation/refusal is rendered through concrete bodily and sensory detail (touch distance, breath, remembered physical habit, immediate stress response) rather than placeholder phrasing.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised 314-327 span contains at least two concrete physical/sensory details and one explicit embodied cost of refusing him, with no euphemistic stand-ins like "that way"; FAIL if the moment still reads as abstract summary.

findings.problem: The meeting runs a long external-only stretch (10+ consecutive paragraphs between interior beats) that thins close-third immersion and makes the chapter read like a transcripted briefing.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:67-91, insert interiority beats tied to Ina's focal experience (micro-sensation, tactical self-correction, memory trigger) at least every 4-6 paragraphs without slowing scene momentum.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the 67-91 span contains at least three distinct Ina-internal beats (thought/sensation/self-assessment) distributed across the exchange so there is no 10+ paragraph interiority gap; FAIL if the passage remains primarily external dialogue/exposition.

findings.problem: Song, Bae, and Park are differentiated by job labels but not by strongly distinct spoken rhythm/idiolect in the core exchange, so the room's pressure dynamics flatten.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:69-89 so each secondary speaker has a clearly distinct verbal pattern (e.g., Song's model-cautious precision, Bae's blunt tactical cynicism, Park's legal narrowing language) while preserving scene content.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a reader can attribute each of Song/Bae/Park lines in 69-89 without dialogue tags based on diction/rhythm alone; FAIL if the lines remain mostly interchangeable committee-speak.

findings.problem: The same facial-microreaction construction (mouth/jaw tightening/twisting to signal emotion) recurs more than twice in one chapter beat, creating a visible prose tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:205-347, keep at most one mouth/jaw microreaction and replace the others with varied embodied cues tied to dock action (movement, voice break, hand behavior, environmental interaction).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised dock scene uses no more than one mouth/jaw tightening-twist reaction and at least two alternate embodied emotional cues; FAIL if three similar mouth/jaw reactions remain.

findings.problem: The ending stacks multiple inbound messages as a rapid hook bundle, which reads outline-forward and diffuses the chapter's final dramatic pressure into notification summary.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:374-377 so one dominant consequence lands in-scene (single urgent demand or interruption) and the chapter closes on a concrete immediate choice/action rather than three sequential message beats.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the closing beat is driven by one primary pressure event that forces an immediate action before the final line; FAIL if the ending remains a multi-message summary stack.

summary: Chapter 11 lands core spine beats and maintains causal momentum, but it fails review due to one CRITICAL diegetic breach (process leakage), one HIGH sanitization retreat at a key intimacy-pressure moment, and additional MEDIUM dialogue/prose/booker issues (interiority drought, voice blur, repeated expression tic, and a hook-stacked ending). Narrative tense remains consistent with past-tense canon, no style-bible example-line copying was found, and no explicit continuity-sheet contradictions were detected.
